jokes.text: All right, now that the popsicle's melted, we've got ourselves a tongue depressor.
jokes.setup: Medical examination setting, expecting proper medical tools
jokes.punchline: Doctor improvises medical equipment from melted candy
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of medical professionalism with absurd improvisation

jokes.text: I get them from prostitutes.
jokes.setup: Patient mentions cold sores from stress
jokes.punchline: Doctor casually admits getting them from prostitutes
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery subverts medical authority with shocking overshare

jokes.text: Sounds like you could use a little R and R, rum and Ritalin.
jokes.setup: Standard medical advice of 'rest and relaxation'
jokes.punchline: Subverted to recommend alcohol and prescription drugs
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that maintains the R&R structure while completely subverting medical ethics

jokes.text: There are four things I want to do this summer, but they're roommates, so it's tricky.
jokes.setup: Patient lists wholesome summer activities
jokes.punchline: Doctor reveals his four things are people in a sexual context
jokes.explanation: Perfect structural parallel that completely subverts wholesome expectations

jokes.text: That's my brother Randy! Tomorrow he's going to jail for the rest of his life.
jokes.setup: Patient confused about pants removal, other doctor mentioned
jokes.punchline: Casually reveals brother is criminal going to prison
jokes.explanation: Absurd casual mention of serious crime fits 30 Rock's style of throwaway darkness

jokes.text: I know that people are feeling sorry for me because my wife was kidnapped by Kim Jong-il
jokes.setup: Character addressing workplace sympathy
jokes.punchline: Reveals absurdly specific and international crisis
jokes.explanation: Perfectly integrates Jack's character with absurd high-stakes personal drama

jokes.text: Hey, I know what you're going through. I once kidnapped a woman.
jokes.setup: Character offering sympathy for kidnapping situation
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was the perpetrator, not victim
jokes.explanation: Classic Tracy Jordan logic - completely misses the point while revealing criminal past

jokes.text: Last night I sat in front of the TV and ate an entire carton of foie gras.
jokes.setup: Character describing depression eating
jokes.punchline: Instead of ice cream, he stress-eats expensive delicacy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack Donaghy character moment - even his depression is wealthy and sophisticated

jokes.text: I can hardly drink my morning-shower Scotch.
jokes.setup: Character describing inability to function normally
jokes.punchline: His normal includes drinking alcohol while showering
jokes.explanation: Instantly quotable line that perfectly encapsulates Jack's excessive lifestyle as casual routine

jokes.text: we would go to Strawberry Fields in Central Park and kick hippies' hacky sacks into the bushes
jokes.setup: Romantic memory of time with wife
jokes.punchline: Their bonding activity was harassing peaceful people
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character work showing how Jack and Avery's elitism manifests as casual cruelty

jokes.text: Normal is a woman and a woman getting married and having a child.
jokes.setup: Character confused about what normal means
jokes.punchline: His idea of normal is same-sex marriage (progressive but wrong context)
jokes.explanation: Shows Jack's confusion and privileged perspective but lands as more confused than funny

jokes.text: Bush is a war criminal! There's so much texting going on these days and no communicating!
jokes.setup: Jack trying to act like a hippie to feel normal
jokes.punchline: His version of hippie complaints are random political/social grievances
jokes.explanation: Jack's attempt to role-play as a hippie shows his complete disconnect from that culture

jokes.text: You can't spell 'America' Without w-o-o-I
jokes.setup: Patriotic song performance
jokes.punchline: Forced inclusion of wool in patriotic anthem
jokes.explanation: Weak wordplay but fits Jenna's desperate attempts to make everything about her current gig

jokes.text: Every year, my aunt sends me a wool sweater for Christmas. We get it, Aunt Alice, you're a sheep.
jokes.setup: Story about relative's predictable gift-giving
jokes.punchline: Treating aunt like she's literally identifying as a sheep
jokes.explanation: Sharp, unexpected escalation of gift annoyance to absurd identity assumption

jokes.text: Paul and I can finally buy that time-share in Betasten Bos, Amsterdam's premier private sex garden.
jokes.setup: Character discussing lucrative endorsement deal
jokes.punchline: Money will fund bizarre sexual tourism investment
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd and specific to Jenna's sexual adventurousness and poor financial priorities

jokes.text: There have genetically-altered men there with minotaur heads that chase you through the brambles.
jokes.setup: Already established bizarre sex garden premise
jokes.punchline: Escalates to mythological genetic modification fantasy
jokes.explanation: Incredibly specific and bizarre escalation that feels both shocking and somehow logical for Jenna

jokes.text: Well, David Eggers and I are designing a new font...
jokes.setup: Writer character asked about summer plans
jokes.punchline: Pretentious literary collaboration on typography
jokes.explanation: Perfect Toofer pretension but predictable for his character type

jokes.text: Last night, for dinner, I put milk in my Apple Jacks.
jokes.setup: Character proving his maturity to girlfriend
jokes.punchline: His version of grown-up behavior is eating cereal properly
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Frank's childish perspective on what constitutes adult behavior

jokes.text: No, I shot my bazooka at the ground to kill myself 'cause Toofer had me cornered.
jokes.setup: Liz thinks Frank died accidentally in game
jokes.punchline: Frank strategically committed suicide to avoid losing
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of gaming obsession and introduces the respawn concept central to episode

jokes.text: I'm wearing a diaper, like a baby would.
jokes.setup: Writers have been gaming for 18 hours straight
jokes.punchline: Frank has abandoned basic human dignity for gaming
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that works for character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Hi, neighbor. I'm Ina Garten. My husband, Jeffrey, is away, and I've got some bruschetta and a white wine opened.
jokes.setup: Liz fantasizing about her Hamptons vacation
jokes.punchline: Dreams of being approached by Food Network celebrity
jokes.explanation: Specific pop culture reference that captures Liz's domestic fantasy life

jokes.text: committing a hate crime against what the city is now claiming was a Jewish tree
jokes.setup: Character casually mentioning legal troubles
jokes.punchline: Absurd hate crime charges against plant with religious identity
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd concept that satirizes both hate crime laws and religious persecution

jokes.text: I was the face of Clinique, a French-Canadian anal-rejuvenation clinic
jokes.setup: Jenna mentioning past endorsement for cosmetics brand
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was actually a sexual medical procedure clinic
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection that plays on brand name recognition while revealing Jenna's desperate career

jokes.text: and I was the feet of FilthyLittleFeet.Com
jokes.setup: Already established inappropriate past endorsements
jokes.punchline: Escalates to fetish website involvement
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Jenna's career desperation, though predictable direction

jokes.text: Paul is a gender-dysmorphic bigenitalian pansexualle
jokes.setup: Explaining Paul's identity to conservative wool executive
jokes.punchline: Over-complicated sexual identity description
jokes.explanation: String of made-up sexual terms that's more absurd than funny

jokes.text: love, warmth, chafed skin
jokes.setup: Listing romantic qualities wool represents
jokes.punchline: Ends with uncomfortable physical side effect
jokes.explanation: Good structure building to unexpected uncomfortable detail

jokes.text: whose parents were killed in front of her while an episode of 'Three's Company' was...
jokes.setup: Warning about wife's traumatic past
jokes.punchline: Trauma specifically connected to innocent sitcom
jokes.explanation: Dark humor connecting innocent TV to trauma, very 30 Rock style

jokes.text: I know your wife was kidnapped by some convenience-store owners
jokes.setup: Kenneth offering sympathy about Jack's situation
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong understanding of North Korea/Kim Jong-il
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth naivety reducing international dictator to local business owner

jokes.text: Mumps, Hill People attacks, cave collapses... both business and residential
jokes.setup: Kenneth explaining how people lose spouses in his hometown
jokes.punchline: Escalates from disease to feudal violence to specific cave distinctions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation revealing Kenneth's absurd rural background with perfect specificity

jokes.text: I like to replace the Union-soldier meat with boiled potatoes
jokes.setup: Kenneth describing family recipe modification
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions cannibalistic Civil War ingredient
jokes.explanation: Incredibly dark throwaway line delivered with Kenneth's innocent cheerfulness - perfect contrast

jokes.text: You two have similar-shaped buttocks
jokes.setup: Jack explaining why Kenneth should sit in Avery's chair
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate physical comparison with his missing wife
jokes.explanation: Appropriately uncomfortable for Jack's grief state but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Deer God, thank you for this venison. Onion God, thank you for these onions. Carrot God, thank you for the carrots.
jokes.setup: Kenneth saying grace before meal
jokes.punchline: Polytheistic thanksgiving to individual food gods
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Kenneth religious confusion creating systematic absurdity with perfect logic

jokes.text: I just bought everything around this house. It's supposed to be a nice area, except for the 'new element' everyone keeps talking about.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining his real estate purchases
jokes.punchline: He doesn't realize he IS the 'new element' people complain about
jokes.explanation: Good character obliviousness about his own disruptive impact on neighborhoods

jokes.text: I got a long night of shooting guns in the air ahead of me
jokes.setup: Tracy casually mentioning evening plans
jokes.punchline: Dangerous and illegal activity treated as routine
jokes.explanation: Classic Tracy casual mention of dangerous behavior as normal routine

jokes.text: If I start screaming in my sleep, do not wake me up. I will attack you.
jokes.setup: Tracy asking to nap at Liz's place
jokes.punchline: Warning about violent sleep disturbances
jokes.explanation: Characteristic Tracy mental health issues delivered as casual warning

jokes.text: Wake me up! Free me from this!
jokes.setup: Previous warning not to wake him from nightmares
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself while having nightmare
jokes.explanation: Quick payoff to setup but predictable contradiction

jokes.text: It was nice to hear a woman's laugh in the house again
jokes.setup: Jack thanking Kenneth for dinner
jokes.punchline: Treating Kenneth as feminine replacement for his wife
jokes.explanation: Jack's grief making him project gender onto Kenneth - uncomfortable but fits character

jokes.text: There's a lot of drawings of Ms. Maroney eating celery that men are giving to her with their hips
jokes.setup: Kenneth explaining why he can't have dinner again
jokes.punchline: Innocent description of obviously sexual graffiti
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth innocence describing obviously sexual content in completely naive terms

jokes.text: My house is full of white cockroaches
jokes.setup: Jack complaining about house full of memories
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's house has different but worse problems
jokes.explanation: Perfectly timed understated response that reveals Kenneth's terrible living conditions

jokes.text: I got you an early anniversary present. It's a new leash for when I walk you through the park like a dog.
jokes.setup: Romantic gesture between partners
jokes.punchline: Gift is for humiliating public sexual roleplay
jokes.explanation: Establishes Paul and Jenna's extreme sexual dynamic that creates main conflict

jokes.text: I'll cancel the sitter for tonight, tell him he won't need to come sit on us after all
jokes.setup: Paul mentioning canceling a sitter
jokes.punchline: Sitter's job is sexual, not childcare
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection playing on normal domestic language for sexual arrangements

jokes.text: Oh, you've just made a very dangerous enemy, Kenneth
jokes.setup: Kenneth taking over Jonathan's cleaning duties
jokes.punchline: Jonathan treats workplace rivalry as serious threat
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Jonathan's dramatic personality

jokes.text: Can I borrow a cup of sugar? I'm trying to get a hummingbird to drink out of my penis
jokes.setup: Normal neighborly request
jokes.punchline: Sugar is for bizarre sexual/animal interaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy line - starts normal then goes completely insane with perfect logic

jokes.text: How many times have I come over and painted your apartment? Three. And by the way, stop doing that.
jokes.setup: Tracy listing things he's done for Liz
jokes.punchline: Liz reveals she never wanted him to paint her apartment
jokes.explanation: Good reveal about Tracy's unwanted 'help' and Liz's inability to stop him

jokes.text: Don't go, Avery. I mean, Kenneth.
jokes.setup: Jack asking Kenneth to stay longer
jokes.punchline: Accidentally calls Kenneth by his wife's name
jokes.explanation: Clear sign of Jack's mental state and growing dependence on Kenneth as replacement

jokes.text: Put on these earrings
jokes.setup: Jack wanting to recreate evening conversations with Avery
jokes.punchline: Forces Kenneth to wear feminine accessories
jokes.explanation: Shows Jack's escalating inappropriate behavior but not particularly funny

jokes.text: I am in the restaurant business, and I really love... golf playing
jokes.setup: Paul trying to seem conventionally masculine
jokes.punchline: Awkward phrasing reveals he's performing unfamiliar masculinity
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Paul's discomfort with conventional masculinity

jokes.text: Oh. Well, I don't have one myself, but I do like a girl with a limp
jokes.setup: Asked about golf handicap
jokes.punchline: Interprets as sexual preference for disabled women
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Paul's complete unfamiliarity with golf culture

jokes.text: My first name is U. Jean, with a U.
jokes.setup: Confusion about woman named Eugene
jokes.punchline: Her actual name is equally absurd abbreviation
jokes.explanation: Absurd name choice that's more weird than funny

jokes.text: Greetings, slaves! Who's ready to get sat on?
jokes.setup: Paul canceling the sitter, but he shows up anyway
jokes.punchline: Sexual service provider arrives at wrong time with inappropriate greeting
jokes.explanation: Payoff to earlier setup but timing saves the dinner plot

jokes.text: That's what I have to do. I can be a normal person. I have to blow myself up.
jokes.setup: Liz seeing the gaming respawn strategy
jokes.punchline: Applies video game logic to real life problems
jokes.explanation: Good connection between A and B plots using central episode metaphor

jokes.text: I put the system on trial. You can't handle the truth!
jokes.setup: Jack refusing to pay fine in court
jokes.punchline: Quotes famous courtroom drama to incompetent judge
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that shows Jack's dramatic nature but isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: I lost my gavel over the weekend, but it doesn't mean you can talk to me like that.
jokes.setup: Judge trying to maintain authority
jokes.punchline: Admits to losing basic tool of office in casual manner
jokes.explanation: Good detail showing institutional incompetence

jokes.text: I have seen 'The Brady Bunch' where Tiger runs away
jokes.setup: Kenneth offering advice about loss
jokes.punchline: Compares wife kidnapping to dog episode of family sitcom
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth inappropriateness treating serious situation with TV logic

jokes.text: God, are you punishing me because my hair is better than yours?
jokes.setup: Jack questioning God about his suffering
jokes.punchline: Assumes God is jealous of his physical appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack vanity turning theological question into personal grooming competition

jokes.text: She once made Rahm Emanuel cry in an airport lounge
jokes.setup: Kenneth describing Avery's meanness
jokes.punchline: Specific example of intimidating notoriously tough political figure
jokes.explanation: Good specific detail that establishes Avery's intimidating nature with political reference

jokes.text: He may be a chinless piece of human garbage, but he will never fill the void that I left behind.
jokes.setup: Kenneth delivering harsh truth as Avery
jokes.punchline: Brutal self-assessment while insulting Kenneth
jokes.explanation: Good character work with Kenneth channeling Avery's mean precision

jokes.text: Hey, Mrs. Dongy, you wan' come on down my birfday party?
jokes.setup: Kenneth dressed as Avery at Jack's request
jokes.punchline: Jonathan treats him like actual Mrs. Donaghy with affected accent
jokes.explanation: Visual payoff to Kenneth's cross-dressing but accent feels forced

jokes.text: Even with your back to me, I can definitely tell it's you
jokes.setup: Eugene addressing Jenna during photo shoot
jokes.punchline: Suggests Jenna is only recognizable from behind
jokes.explanation: Mild visual gag about Jenna's appearance but not particularly clever

jokes.text: You wanted us to be normal? Well, this is our normal, and this is what we think of your morality clause.
jokes.setup: Paul finally standing up to wool council pressure
jokes.punchline: Defiantly embraces their sexual unconventionality
jokes.explanation: Good character moment but the defiance speech is predictable

jokes.text: Give Mommy some beard. Oh, you've got some crumbs in here. Oh, eat it.
jokes.setup: Paul and Jenna's public display
jokes.punchline: Intimate grooming becomes unexpectedly disgusting
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that goes too far into genuinely disgusting territory

jokes.text: Good God! Lemon? Liddy, say, 'Good God, Lemon.' Guh gah, Lem.
jokes.setup: Jack seeing Liz on chain gang
jokes.punchline: Teaching baby to say his catchphrase about Liz
jokes.explanation: Sweet callback that shows Jack bonding with daughter over shared Liz exasperation

jokes.text: I'm outdoors, I'm wearing comfortable clothes, I'm gardening, and I'm learning Spanish.
jokes.setup: Liz explaining how she got her dream vacation
jokes.punchline: Achieving goals through community service/punishment
jokes.explanation: Good callback with ironic achievement of goals through opposite means

jokes.text: where she spat up on a white lady's dreads
jokes.setup: Jack describing trip to Strawberry Fields with baby
jokes.punchline: Baby performs version of Jack and Avery's hippie harassment
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing the baby carrying on family tradition of hippie harassment

jokes.text: There you are! Bad dog! You'll go in the crate tonight.
jokes.setup: Paul finding Jenna after she lost her wool endorsement
jokes.punchline: Treats her like actual dog as promised earlier
jokes.explanation: Payoff to earlier setup but fairly predictable

jokes.text: Some idiot rammed his boat that I was driving into it
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining house damage
jokes.punchline: Calls someone else an idiot for his own destructive behavior
jokes.explanation: Classic Tracy logic avoiding responsibility while blaming others

jokes.text: No, I see you. I'm killing myself. Respawn.
jokes.setup: Writers still playing the same game
jokes.punchline: Frank continues respawn strategy even when directly addressed
jokes.explanation: Consistent with character obsession but not particularly surprising
